LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS KIM POPOVITS, JIM VAUGHN, BRAD COLE, KIM MCEACHRON AND GENOMIC HEALTH INC'S MOTION TO TRANSFER ACTION, CHANGE OF VENUE IS GRANTED. THE CASE IS TRANSFERRED TO SAN MATEO COUNTY AND PLAINTIFF IS REQUIRED TO PAY ALL COSTS OF THE TRANSFER. MS. JUNGMAN HAS NOT SHOWN THAT SAN FRANCISCO IS A PROPER VENUE FOR THIS CASE PER GOVERNMENT CODE 12965(B) AND DEFENDANTS HAVE SHOWN THAT IT IS NOT. NO EVIDENCE HAS BEEN PRESENTED THAT ANY UNLAWFUL PRACTICE UNDER FEHA WAS COMMITTED IN SAN FRANCISCO OR THAT THE RECORDS RELEVANT TO THE ALLEGED UNLAWFUL FEHA PRACTICES ARE LOCATED IN THIS COUNTY OR THAT MS. JUNGMAN WOULD HAVE WORKED IN SAN FRANCISCO OR WOULD HAVE ACCESS TO PUBLIC ACCOMMODATIONS IN SAN FRANCISCO BUT FOR THE ALLEGED FEHA UNLAWFUL PRACTICES. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976.

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-22-2016 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Transfer Action (Change Of Venue)
Off Calendar May-05-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Krista Baughman of Dhillon Law Group Inc. telephone number 415-433-1700 for Plaintiff Aimee Jungman.
John Baum of Hirschfeld Kraemer LLP telephone number 415-835-9006 for Defendant Genomic Health Inc.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS KIM POPOVITS, JIM VAUGHN, BRAD COLE, KIM MCEACHRON and GENOMIC HEALTH INC'S Motion To Transfer Action, Change Of Venue is granted. The case is transferred to San Mateo County and plaintiff is required to pay all costs of the transfer. Ms. Jungman has not shown that San Francisco is a proper venue for this case per Government Code 12965(b) and defendants have shown that it is not. No evidence has been presented that any unlawful practice under FEHA was committed in San Francisco or that the records relevant to the alleged unlawful FEHA practices are located in this county or that Ms. Jungman would have worked in San Francisco or would have access to public accommodations in San Francisco but for the alleged FEHA unlawful practices. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)
